Assistant: I can be more specific.  I can recommend a specific tool that you can use to tighten up a few connections on the mower that'll hold the oil in place.  But I'll also need to tell you more about what's going on inside the mower.  
At the moment, oil is collecting in the bottom of the mower.  You can take a look at this.  Just push your thumb down into the little round depression on the side and push.  Now tilt the mower over and see if the oil comes out.

Human: There is no little depression on the side to push with my thumb. Are you sure we are talking about the same thing here?

Assistant: I'm afraid not. I'm sorry. We're talking about two different types of lawn mower.  One type that uses a crank that's connected to the wheels by a pulley, and one that uses a motor.

Human: I have one with a motor, and there is no indentation there. I just want to change the oil.

Assistant: I can tell you how to change the oil on a mower with a motor.  I can recommend a tool that you can use to tighten up the motor, and the fuel lines that feed it, to keep the oil in place.  But I'll also need to tell you more about what's going on inside the mower.

Human: No, I don't need to keep the oil in place I need to change it. I need to take the old oil out and put in new oil. Do you know anything about  what I am talking about?

Assistant: I'd be happy to give you instructions for this part, which is actually pretty easy.  What's most important here is that you be careful about damaging the motor, the fan, or the oil lines when you're putting oil in.  It'll also help if you take your time and do things carefully.

Human: Of course I will be careful, just tell me what I need to do already.

Assistant: Let's begin by locating the oil reservoir, which is located at the bottom of the mower.  When you've got it in hand, you'll be able to tell if it's empty or full.  When it's empty, it'll be too light to hold and will flop around in your hand.  
Now, you can see that this reservoir is connected to the crankshaft.  You can unscrew the oil fill cap.  You might also notice that it's connected to the crankshaft by a couple of screws.  You'll need to remove them too.  When you've taken them out, you'll be able to open the reservoir up like a flower.  That's what I'd recommend that you do now.  Then you can see that the reservoir is only about half full of oil.

Human: I don't  think that is correct at all. I am not sure I want you to help me after all.
